text: Fill in the blanks on lipid chemistry, metabolism, and clinical significance.
questions: Beta-oxidation of fatty acids occurs in the *mitochondria/mitochondrial matrix* and produces *acetyl-CoA/Acetyl CoA*, NADH, and FADH2 in each cycle.
The rate-limiting enzyme of cholesterol synthesis is *HMG-CoA reductase/HMGCR*, which is the target of *statins/Statins* — the most widely prescribed lipid-lowering drugs.

*LDL/Low-density lipoprotein* is called bad cholesterol because it deposits cholesterol in arterial walls, while *HDL/High-density lipoprotein* is called good cholesterol because it performs *reverse cholesterol transport*.
Fatty acids are transported into the mitochondria by the *carnitine/Carnitine* shuttle system; the rate-limiting enzyme is *CPT-1/carnitine palmitoyltransferase 1/CPT1*.
Lipids are classified into simple lipids (fats, waxes), *compound/complex* lipids (phospholipids, glycolipids), and *derived/Derived* lipids (cholesterol, steroids).
